about.title: About
about.description: Freelance senior full-stack and TypeScript engineer (React, Vue, Angular) on the Costa Blanca, Spain. 25 years shipping for the web through JiggyBit S.L.
about.body:
When I started building for the web, "frontend" was where you were put before you'd earned the backend. The reward had a ceiling, so I went full-stack early.

Twenty-five years later the industry has cycled twice. Frontend became a specialism: the people who could build fast, polished single-page apps were suddenly the ones in demand. Then AI tooling shifted the equation again. The cost of going deep on both ends at once dropped, and the generalists who never stopped writing backend code found themselves with an edge. This site is one piece of that: I built it to the same standard in React, Vue, and Angular, on one backend I wrote to serve all three. The deeper backend, data, and infrastructure work is on the timeline.

In all those years I've never been handed a problem I couldn't ship my way through. Different stacks, different eras, the same habit: get the shape of the problem right, then build the simplest thing that meets the requirements.

I care about code that's correct, tested, and legible. I lean on static analysis and tests that cover the code paths, and I reach for a pattern when it removes more complexity than it adds, not because a convention insists on it. Elegance, to me, is the absence of cleverness you don't need.

At a number of places where I wasn't hired as a lead, I was asked to become one. The work I'm proudest of is quieter 
than that though: mentoring people, passing on what I've learned, and leaving a team better than I found it.

I took the long way here. I started out in the Netherlands, moved to New Zealand and kept my clients on from there, then spent eleven years in Australia, first in Brisbane and later in Melbourne. A lot of those years were inside agencies, where you learn to ship fast across wildly different clients and codebases. Consulting came after that, and six years ago I went out on my own, back in Europe.

These days I'm on the Costa Blanca in Spain, working through my own company, JiggyBit S.L. The traveling was never only about the job. I like experiencing different places and cultures, and this corner of Spain has everything else I value: space, mountains, long views, and sun. Outside client work, I'm often deep in my own systematic trading system, or fixing up an old Spanish house that needs about as much debugging as the code does.

The timeline has the full story, role by role, and the whole site is open source, so you can read every line.

services.title: Services
services.description: Senior full-stack engineering and technical leadership across React, Vue, Angular, and TypeScript backends: systems thinking, team quality, sound decisions, and tech-debt strategy.
services.body:
I work as a senior full-stack engineer and technical lead, usually embedded in a team that needs to move faster without breaking what already works. The framework matters less than the judgment around it, and judgment is where I add the most.

Most problems I am handed are not really about the code in front of me. They are about how the parts fit: the data model, the boundaries between services, the path a request actually takes. I map that first, then build the simplest thing that meets the requirement. Systems thinking like this is what produces architecture that survives changing requirements instead of buckling under them.

Quality only holds if the whole team can meet the same bar. I set up the static analysis, tests, and real coverage that make correct, legible code the default, I review with care, and I mentor as I go. At several places I was asked to lead where I had not been hired to, usually because the work around me had become more reliable.

The expensive mistakes are made at the whiteboard, not the keyboard. A premature abstraction, the wrong storage engine, a migration sequenced badly: each one costs months once it ships. I am the person in the room who asks the awkward question before the commitment is locked in, and who would rather prove the cheaper path with a small spike than argue it on a slide.

Not all technical debt is worth paying down. I help teams tell the difference, rank what is genuinely slowing delivery, and fold the repayment into normal feature work so the codebase gets healthier without a stop-the-world rewrite.

In practice that spans frontend architecture and design systems, performance auditing and optimisation, full-stack TypeScript development across React, Vue, and Angular, and framework migration and modernisation. If that sounds like the gap on your team, get in touch.
